launcher_stampede_sh.detail: No timeout check for commanders in stampede.sh. Launcher has COPILOT_PREFLIGHT_TIMEOUT (line 25) for startup preflight only, not for ongoing stuck detection.

monitor_stampede_monitor_sh.detail: Monitor checks claimed/*.json age > 120s and alerts with bell (lines 201-216). Also has orphan recovery after 180s (ORPHAN_RECOVERY_AFTER, line 25) for claimed tasks when all agents are dead.

stuck_agent_detection.detail: Monitor does NOT have a dedicated blocked/stuck banner for commander panes. Generic stuck detection applies to all claimed tasks but does not differentiate commander from worker roles.

failed_commander_recovery.detail: stampede.sh does not retry failed commander launches. On failure (exit code != 0), it logs to child-agents.jsonl and swarm-state.json with status=failed (lines 983-1037) but does not relaunch.

failed_worker_recovery.detail: Workers also do not have retry logic in launcher. The monitor's orphan recovery (lines 237-278) returns claimed tasks to queue when all agents are dead and claim_age >= 180s, but this requires a new agent to pick up the task.

commander_active_gate.detail: Orphan recovery checks if commander is actively heartbeating via swarm-state.json before returning claimed task to queue (lines 247-272). Threshold is ORPHAN_RECOVERY_AFTER (180s).

retry_budget_field.usage: Context capsule constraints only (line 210); no actual retry logic in launcher or monitor consumes this value.

teardown_behavior.detail: --teardown (lines 302-362) kills PIDs and tmux session, then sets state.json phase=torn_down. Does NOT drain in-flight bundle writers or wait for atomic writes to complete. Does NOT update status field.
teardown_behavior.gap: MAJOR: Teardown may leave partial/corrupt results if writers are mid-write. Uses phase=torn_down but state.json status field remains unchanged.

duplicate_run_id_handling.detail: Lines 617-622: if tmux session exists, it logs a warning then kills the existing session. Does NOT refuse to proceed or require confirmation. Overwrites .stampede/{run_id}/ contents.
duplicate_run_id_handling.gap: MAJOR: Duplicate run_id silently kills prior session and overwrites data. No --force flag required.

pid_capture.detail: Lines 1162-1192: PID capture happens once after 'sleep 5'. Uses find_leaf_pid() to walk process tree (lines 71-88), but no refresh on pane restart. If agent hasn't spawned yet, captures wrong PID.
pid_capture.gap: Race condition: 5-second fixed sleep may not be enough for slow agent startups; PID is never refreshed.

pane_death_handling.detail: stampede-monitor.sh (lines 127-142, 221-232) checks tmux pane status via list-panes and #{pane_dead}. Same logic for commanders and workers - no role-specific handling. Commander pane death updates icon to '✕' and word='dead' but no separate blocked state banner.

gaps.description: --teardown does not drain in-flight writers; sets phase=torn_down but not status=stopped
Duplicate run_id silently kills prior tmux session and overwrites .stampede/{run_id}/ without requiring --force
PID capture is one-shot after fixed 5s sleep with no refresh; race condition with slow agent startups
No dedicated blocked/stuck banner for commanders vs workers in monitor
retry_budget field in commander capsule (value=1) is never consumed by launcher or monitor
Monitor orphan recovery exists (180s threshold + commander-active gate) but requires new agent to claim returned task

evidence: commander-001/bundle.json - prior QA confirmed these same findings

cross_validation.atm-006-001_alignment: Fully aligned - same 2 blockers (teardown + duplicate run_id) identified
cross_validation.additional_finding: PID capture race condition not explicitly called out in atm-006-001
